mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns are negative in greater than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9 percent, consumer spending power contracts sharply, reducing corporate revenue expectations and forcing equity prices lower over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds the 9.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which compounds corporate profit forecasts and drives positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive monthly equity returns during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of greater than 0.5 percent fail to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery periods with a t-test p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of weeks following CPIAUCSL acceleration above the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real GDP confirms broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing earnings momentum and generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Co-movement of PAYEMS and GDPC1 yields negative quarterly returns in greater than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales reflect weakening consumer demand, which directly reduces corporate revenue projections and forces equity prices lower over the next month during contractionary phases.
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS growth fails to predict negative 20-day returns during contraction periods with a Rank IC above -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production signal immediate improvements in factory output and supply chain throughput, generating positive weekly equity returns through earnings revision channels.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes produce negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.
